introSection: White Cyclone (Japanese: ホワイトサイクロン) was an Intamin wooden roller coaster located at Nagashima Spa Land in Kuwana, Mie, Japan.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
White Cyclone opened in 1994.

In October 2017, Nagashima Spa Land announced that the ride would be closing on 28 January 2018 and be replaced with a Rocky Mountain Construction I-Box roller coaster named Hakugei in 2019.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains where built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters.

fullText:
White Cyclone (Japanese: ホワイトサイクロン) was an Intamin wooden roller coaster located at Nagashima Spa Land in Kuwana, Mie, Japan.

White Cyclone opened in 1994.

In October 2017, Nagashima Spa Land announced that the ride would be closing on 28 January 2018 and be replaced with a Rocky Mountain Construction I-Box roller coaster named Hakugei in 2019.



Unpainted track and white supports.

The trains where built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters.
